resources.string: Today\'s Rest, Charge and Effort, with live heart rate and strap battery at a glance.
Compact Rest, Charge and Effort widget, with live heart rate and strap battery.
NOOP widget couldn\'t draw — it will recover on the next update.
Please read the points below, then confirm each statement.
NOOP is an unofficial project: not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by WHOOP, Inc. \"WHOOP\" is their trademark, used only to name the hardware NOOP works with.
Using NOOP may breach WHOOP\'s Terms of Service
Use it only with a device you own, to read your own data. Whether to use it (and any effect on your WHOOP account, subscription, device, or warranty) is your decision, and your risk alone.

NOOP talks to your strap\'s firmware over an unofficial, independently-mapped protocol. There is a residual risk to the device, its data, and its connection to official services. You assume that risk.
Not a medical device, not medical advice
Every metric is an unvalidated approximation. Don\'t use NOOP to diagnose, treat, or make any health decision. Always consult a qualified professional.

NOOP is free and provided \"as is\", with no warranty. Liability is limited to the maximum extent the law that applies to you allows, and nothing here removes protections your local law won\'t let us remove.
The full terms are in TERMS.md, shipped with NOOP. This is not legal advice.
I am not a WHOOP employee, contractor, or affiliate, and I am not using NOOP on WHOOP\'s behalf.

I own the WHOOP device I will use with NOOP and will only use it to access my own data. Doing so is my decision and my risk, including any effect on my WHOOP account, subscription, device, or warranty, and it may breach WHOOP\'s Terms of Service.
I understand NOOP is unofficial and experimental, is provided free and \"as is\" with no warranty, and is not a medical device or medical advice.
To the fullest extent the law allows, I will not hold the NOOP project or its contributors liable for any loss or damage arising from my use of it.
I have read and accept these terms, and I\'m using NOOP with my own device and my own data, at my own risk.
The thread of you over time.
Step to another week with the arrows above to see its review.
Each bar is one day\'s Charge score, low to high. The 53-week calendar heat-grid is part of the desktop app.
Not enough data for this window.
Trends need history to draw. Import your WHOOP export in Data Sources to see weeks, months and years instantly.
Import your history first. A WHOOP export in Data Sources fills every metric you can explore here in about a minute.
Every second of your day, zoomable.
No %1$s recorded yet. Sync your strap to populate this trend.
Only one reading in range — widen the window to see a trend.
Every second of your day. Drag back up to 3 days.
Pinch to zoom · drag to pan
Nothing offloaded for this window yet.
Other sources don\'t offload raw per-second data on-device.

Ask for a faster Bluetooth radio mode while your strap hands over its stored history. Sends the same data in less airtime, so it may sync sooner and use a little less strap battery — but your strap has to support it, and it trades some range for speed. New and unproven: if syncing gets flaky when your phone is further away, turn it back off.

Ask Android for a faster Bluetooth link while your strap hands over its stored history, so a big backlog catches up sooner. Only during the sync itself — your live heart rate and the overnight stream are untouched. New and unproven: if it doesn\'t help, or costs more battery than it\'s worth, turn it back off and say so on the issue tracker.
Allowed — your phone won\'t stop NOOP\'s overnight sync to save battery. The permission itself uses no battery; what uses it is the overnight sync you switched on above.

Your phone (%1$s) can stop background apps to save battery, which can make NOOP miss overnight sleep and recovery data. Turn this on to whitelist NOOP.\n\nAndroid will ask “Stop optimising battery usage?” and warn that NOOP may drain your battery — that\'s its standard wording for any app allowed to run in the background. Tap Allow. The battery goes on the overnight sync you already switched on above; this only stops Android cutting it short.

Some phones can stop background apps to save battery, which can make NOOP miss overnight sleep and recovery data. Turn this on to whitelist NOOP.\n\nAndroid will ask “Stop optimising battery usage?” and warn that NOOP may drain your battery — that\'s its standard wording for any app allowed to run in the background. Tap Allow. The battery goes on the overnight sync you already switched on above; this only stops Android cutting it short.
Ease the load on your strap when its battery is running low. The strap keeps banking data on its own, so nothing is lost — NOOP just talks to it less often to help it last until you can charge it.
Slows background strap-sync (every 45 min instead of 15) while your strap\'s battery is low. No data loss — the strap banks everything, so sync just batches into larger, less frequent pulls.

While your strap\'s battery is low, stop the always-on background HRV stream — the biggest continuous drain on the strap. A Live screen still shows heart rate, and it re-arms automatically once the strap is charged.
A WHOOP strap bonds to a single device. While it\'s connected to NOOP it won\'t stream
Advanced: I already have my ring\'s key
Beta. * is an on-device estimate. Skin temp is a trend versus your own baseline, steps
Both NOOP and the Oura app can use a ring you own by key, but only one can hold the Bluetooth link at a time.
Experimental, best-effort support. We\'re still testing these, so they might not connect on every device. They never make up data, and they\'ll tell you honestly when live isn\'t possible.
I understand this disconnects the ring from Oura and that NOOP cannot undo it for me. To go back to Oura I would factory-reset the ring again and set it up in the Oura app.
I understand this disconnects the ring from Oura and that NOOP cannot undo it for me.
Installing NOOP\'s key and confirming the ring answers only to NOOP. Keep the ring close and do not open the Oura app.
Keep the Oura app instead (import a file)
Make sure it\'s awake and not connected elsewhere.
NOOP stores this key only on this device, in the same place it stores your paired bands.
Not showing up? Make sure you reset the ring in the Oura app and force-quit it, then tap Rescan. A ring still owned by Oura will not list here.
That is not a 32-character hex key.
The ring is not bricked. To go back to where you started, factory-reset it again and set it
We could not take over this ring.
WHOOP 5.0 / MG support is newer and still experimental in NOOP.
WHOOP is NOOP\'s primary, fully-supported band. Other heart-rate straps stream live heart rate and HRV, but not WHOOP\'s deeper sleep and recovery data.
Each day uses the time above unless you set a different one here.
No naps to review. Detected naps show up here after a history sync.
Notifications are off, so this can\'t buzz yet. Turn on the master switch in
Notify on low and full battery
Newest first. Restoring replaces this device\'s data.
Older backups beyond this many are pruned, oldest first (≈ that many days of
Replace all current data with %1$s? This cannot be undone.
Replace this device\'s data with one of your backups. This overwrites current data,
Roughly when the daily backup runs (best-effort — the system may slide it a little).
Tip: a desktop Drive / Dropbox app auto-syncs a chosen folder. On the phone, save to a
Writes a fresh dated backup to your folder once a day at the time below, keeping
A relaxation rhythm, not cardiac control. It never paces below a safe rate and you can stop anytime. If your heart rate doesn\'t settle, we\'ll say so plainly.
Connect your strap. Calm me is a felt rhythm on the wrist, so it needs a bonded connection.
Connect your strap for haptic guidance. You\'ll feel one pulse on the inhale, two on the exhale, so you can breathe with your eyes closed.
Estimate from PPG-derived R-R: relaxation guidance, not a clinical reading. Your pace drifts, so we date it and you can re-measure anytime.
Estimate only: a higher RMSSD while paced usually means your parasympathetic "rest" branch is engaging. It is not a clinical reading; trends over a session matter more than any single number.

Everyone has a breathing pace (usually between 4.5 and 7 breaths a minute) where the heart\'s rhythm swings the most with each breath. We pace you through a few candidate paces, measure how your HRV responds, and lock the one that resonates best for you.
Locked %1$s. Switch to Breathe to use it, or re-measure above.
Not enough clean beat data to lock a pace today. Try again rested, sitting still with the strap snug. For now we\'ll pace you at 5.5 br/min (coherence).
Relaxation guidance from your own numbers: not a health alert, and not a diagnosis. Trends matter more than any single number.
Sit still and breathe with the buzz. You can stop anytime; a stopped sweep won\'t lock a pace.
Soft tone on each phase · honours silent mode
Started at %1$s bpm · the rhythm trails your heart down.
That\'s normal. A paced breath often settles things when a metronome alone doesn\'t.
The strap buzzes a gentle rhythm just below your current heart rate, a felt metronome to relax toward. It trails your heart down rather than yanking it, and stops on its own.
Waiting for a resting heart rate. Start a live reading first, or come back when you\'re still.
Your HRV dipped while you were still. Want a minute to breathe?
After your %1$s cutoff - may still be active at bed.
Flag drinks late enough to still be active at bedtime.
Have your last caffeine by about %1$s to clear most of it
Log a coffee, tea, or energy drink and NOOP shows a rough estimate of how much may
Ask anything about your recent recovery, strain, sleep or HRV.
Enter any model id the provider accepts.
Let the coach use my data
Compare needs at least two metrics with history
Not enough overlapping days between these metrics in %1$s. Widen the range.
Acts as a standard Bluetooth heart-rate strap. Pair NOOP from your treadmill,
Broadcast heart rate as a Bluetooth sensor
Broadcast HR is ON. Your strap is advertising its heart rate continuously,
Re-pull new Health Connect data (e.g. Samsung Health → Health Connect) each
Share computed metrics back to Health Connect
This permanently deletes everything imported from Apple Health: heart rate, HRV,
Write the metrics NOOP computes from your strap (resting HR, HRV, SpO₂,

Two independent protocol tables disagree on the extended-battery opcode (98 vs 87). This sends the curated read-only 98 and dumps the strap\'s full raw reply to the strap log. A battery-style payload confirms 98 on your firmware; a short stub means it stays ambiguous. Nothing is written to the strap. Please export and share the strap log afterwards.
Paired locally. NOOP owns this ring while it holds the key. If you reset it again or set it
The WHOOP 4.0 reboot frame isn\'t confirmed — a normal Restart is ignored (#235).
WHOOP is NOOP\'s primary, fully-supported band. Other heart-rate straps are an early,
You removed your active strap. Choose which paired band provides your live data, or
Fused on %1$s. Nothing leaves it: no account, no cloud.
NOOP picks the best-sourced number and shows you where each came from. It\'s for wellness and curiosity. It doesn\'t diagnose or replace medical advice.
NOOP shows the %1$s reading because it %2$s for this metric: a higher-trust source here, not a verdict that the others are wrong.
Your bands report different numbers. Here\'s every source, and the one NOOP is using.
± 5 yr · a fitness comparison, not a biological age
A wellness estimate from your habits, not a clinical biological age.
Baselines learned on-device over 14 days. Bars read each signal against a
Cycle phase, body-clock and illness heads-up are approximations computed on your device from
How accurate is this Fitness Age?
Longer ranges unlock as more history builds.
Not enough history in this range
One reading so far — your trend chart fills in here once a second
NOOP never makes up a number. When it can\'t compute one honestly it tells you
NOOP never shows you a number it had to make up. If a score isn\'t ready,
Sleep · scores · recording · where your numbers come from
An HRV reading needs the live R-R stream. Open the Live screen and connect your strap,
Not enough clean beats - sit still and try again. %1$s of
A simple goal that adjusts to your effort. General wellness guidance, not medical advice.
Enter any amount from 1 to %1$s ml.
No drinks logged yet. Tap Sip, Cup or Bottle to start.
Sip %1$s ml · Cup %2$s ml · Bottle %3$s ml
“Dose” here is timing (later in the day = stronger), not milligrams.
Everything here is a pattern in your own logged days: an association with an
Log alcohol or late caffeine with an amount and NOOP fits a personal dose curve:
Not enough overlap between your journal answers and
Log at least one behaviour above before starting an experiment.
Not enough overlap between your journal answers and
Not enough overlapping history to correlate your metrics yet.
Pick one behaviour you log, one outcome, and a short window. NOOP
Ranked, lag-aware: which of your habits actually move your Charge, plus your
Tag a few sessions of the same activity and NOOP will learn its personal recovery cost.
What moves you. Ranked patterns in your own data, and your dose-response.
Charge weighs your heart-rate variability against your personal baseline
Estimate from today\'s effort, your typical sleep and your %1$s-night
No scored days in this window. Widen the range or import more history.
No scored days yet. Sync your strap to collect raw streams. Effort and Rest are
You\'ll likely wake around %1$s ± %2$s Charge if you sleep about
Bond your strap on the Live screen to feel the transitions hands-free.
Pause to change work, rest, or rounds.
History stays under the original question so WHOOP imports still line up.
A private notebook, not a medical service.
All stays on this phone. Nothing is sent anywhere.
Bring in a markers CSV (date, marker, value, unit). Names that match the catalog
Choose a wearable signal to compare
It\'s a notebook, not a lab. NOOP lines up the numbers you enter. It doesn\'t test,
Lab Book is a private notebook, not a medical service. NOOP stores and lines up the numbers
%1$s readings line up, but there isn\'t enough variation to compute a relationship.
%1$s readings used · %2$s %3$s association. This is your own data
Pick a wearable signal (resting HR, HRV, sleep, Charge, weight…) to line it up against this
Read the full Lab Book note
These are your own numbers shown back to you. NOOP doesn\'t decide whether any value is
Type in a blood-pressure reading or a cholesterol value from your last appointment.
Scan and connect to start a live stream.
Session ring. %1$s. Long-press to show heart rate.
The strap signal was gone for 10 minutes, so the session ended itself.
This stops recording and saves what\'s captured so far. It can\'t be resumed.
Entered together; stored as two markers so each lines up cleanly against your signals.
Lab Book keeps your own numbers - it doesn\'t test, read, or judge them, and it\'s not medical
No match. Add it as a custom marker below.
NOOP never fills this in - it only shows back exactly what you type from your own report.
Type in a number from your own report. It stays on this phone.
Mood correlations unlock after %1$s days of
Not enough overlapping history to correlate mood with your
One check-in per day; picking another face overwrites today\'s.
Phone permission was denied, so phone-call buzzing is off.
Uses the same quiet-hours and worn-only rules.
Wrist delivery needs Notification Access so NOOP can read which apps notify
A private window into your recovery, sleep and strain. Read straight from your strap, kept only on this phone.
A quick word before you connect
all your data, none of the cloud
Every beat, every night, every day, woven into one quiet picture of you. Welcome to NOOP.
Health Connect is not available on this device.
No WHOOP? You can still continue. Pair a heart-rate strap (Polar, Wahoo, Coospo, Garmin HRM…)
This can run while you finish setup

During quiet, still, resting windows, NOOP looks at the timing between your heartbeats (R-R intervals) and draws their Poincaré scatter. From the cloud it computes its short and long axes (SD1, SD2) and a few plain regularity numbers. Movement and noisy windows are skipped, not shown. These are transparent, published descriptive statistics: a picture of your timing, never a clinical measurement.
Each dot pairs one heartbeat interval with the next. A tight line along the diagonal means a steady beat; a rounder, more spread-out cloud means the timing varied more.

Experimental wellness visualization: not a diagnosis, not an ECG, and not a medical device. It cannot detect any heart condition. Beat-to-beat variation has many ordinary, benign causes. If you feel unwell or are worried, contact a qualified professional; in an emergency, your local emergency service. Everything is computed on your device.
Every score carries a small honesty label. Calibrating means NOOP is still
How sure is NOOP? · Solid · Building · Calibrating
NOOP gives you three daily scores (Charge, Effort and Rest), each on a 0-100
These are independent approximations from a consumer strap, built on open science: not
NOOP can read a coarse menstrual-cycle phase from your nightly skin temperature,
Hours vs Needed progress bar, %1$s percent
Logged as a nap. Wrong? Tap Edit to adjust your sleep and wake times.
No movement detail for this night.
No nights with sleep data yet. Your ledger fills in as you wear the strap to bed.
Not enough history in this range. Try 3M, 6M, or ALL.
Sleep consistency nightly bed and wake chart
Tap when you\'re heading to bed or when you wake. Each tap is logged with the time. It doesn\'t change tonight\'s detected sleep.
This metric needs at least two nights of data.
Why this is logged as a nap
Why this is your main sleep
A backup alarm is set for %1$s. It fires even if Bluetooth drops, the strap isn\'t worn, or NOOP is closed.
NOOP doesn\'t have permission to set exact alarms, so your wake isn\'t guaranteed.
The earliest NOOP will wake you.
The guaranteed alarm fires this long after your earliest time.
This is a coarse cue from heart rate, not a clinical sleep-stage reading. If the strap
Turn on the smart alarm to wake inside a window you choose.
Wake me with a smart alarm
Wake me with a strap buzz
While you\'re inside the window, NOOP watches your live heart rate from the strap. Deep
Your WHOOP 5/MG won\'t arm this until Experimental mode is on (Settings →
No days yet where both NOOP and your phone counted steps. Once your phone logs a
NOOP estimates your steps from your WHOOP\'s motion, calibrated to your phone\'s step
On the days your phone also counted steps, NOOP learns how much your motion maps to
Open NOOP near your strap and let it catch up (a full history sync can take a while on
Override the automatic fit with your own steps-per-motion value. Useful if your phone
Takes effect on the next analytics pass (after the next sync).
These are the days where your phone also counted steps, so NOOP can learn how your
These days are excluded from the estimate (your phone\'s real count is shown instead).
We\'re not seeing any motion from your strap yet. Steps are estimated from your WHOOP\'s
Reading your heart-rate variability and resting heart rate…
Your last %1$s hours have stayed in the high band. A few minutes
Also write the strap log to the system log (logcat) for development over adb. Off by default.
Android runs this via WorkManager (Doze may delay it).
Heads up: this test mode is off, so the report has no capture for it. For a
Report a bug with my log
This is exactly what your report will contain. Nothing leaves this phone until you tap Share.
This restarts the roughly 4-night build-up for Charge and your HRV baseline. Your history stays.
No new nights from your strap for %1$d days. Check it\'s connected and saving data.
Choose which cards show on Today and reorder them with the arrows.
Hold a section and drag it to reorder — here, or directly on the Today cards.
How Charge is calculated. The method behind the score.
See how Charge, Effort and Rest are calculated, and how they differ from WHOOP.
The method behind the score, not today\'s values.
Workout in progress, %1$s, %2$s. Return to workout.
New release notes and fresh data will land here.
NOOP pairs with the WHOOP 4.0 and the WHOOP 5.0/MG. They share most of what matters (live
What each can read, and why
You\'re not missing the broadcast feature. To share your heart rate with a gym machine,
Avg HR is shown as typed. The HR graph, zones and Effort stay from the recorded session.
Enter a sport, a positive duration (≤ 24h), and valid HR (25-250) / calories (0-20,000).
Share of imported zone time, duration-weighted across sessions (approximate).
The average above was edited. The graph, zones and Effort stay from the recorded session.
These sessions have no sport label yet. Pick one for the merged session.
This session\'s contribution to the day\'s Effort, as captured during the workout.
Make %1$s your active device now? It will provide your live data. You can change
NOOP will install its own key on the ring and become its owner. The Oura app will no longer control this ring. This is intended and it cannot be undone from NOOP.
5/MG bonding on Android + Health Monitor fix
A big bug-fix sweep, with the Test Centre to back it up
A big one: smarter sleep, naps, more devices, and a load of fixes
A calmer Today, your Charge explained, and new HRV science under the hood
A cleaner home - refreshed design, a new inbox, your photo
A fresh look - new gold-on-navy icon
A round of fixes - reconnect, exports & Health setup
A round of fixes - steps, Insights & Health setup
A smart wake alarm, live workout mode, an editable Today, and lifting imports
A smoother dashboard on big histories, and a clearer Smart Alarm
A sync chip on Today, clearer strap-clock warnings, and complete German
A WHOOP-style Today chart, clearer sleep times - and a big iPhone update
Accuracy, reliability & accessibility - a big community-fixes wave
Android: faster sync, skin temp, sync status, alarm groundwork
Android: notification recovery fix + widget armour
Android reconnect guide + a startup-crash fix
Android: reliable reconnect after a dropout
Android: share back to Health Connect
Android: the widget now actually updates
Android: WHOOP 4 on newer firmware now records data
Apple Health export, sleep nights recovered, and imported-ride Effort
Background GPS, sleep-time editing, log-ahead, and a sharper Rest tile
Backup controls, and a lighter app
Backup restore fixed, plus appearance controls
Backup & Restore, the wrong-day fix, and a smarter Test Centre
Battery + responsiveness: smarter sync, lighter notification
Better diagnostics for newer strap firmware - so we can decode it
Big fix wave - clock, reconnect, local LLM, Explore, weight and more
Bluetooth stream + Apple Health sync fixes
Board Sweep: battery days-left, browse past weeks, breathing cues, and a pile of fixes
Broadcast your heart rate to Garmin, Zwift and gym kit
Browse past nights, smarter Coach, workout times, battery & more
Browse the last few days, interactive charts, and a Vital Signs screen (Android)
Bug-fix sweep: steps, sleep export, and a battery-saving reconnect fix
Bug fixes: Effort, the widget\'s day, and Oura reconnect
Buzz WHOOP 4 in the Smart Alarm
Charge, Effort & Rest - NOOP\'s own scores, out of 100
Charge shows "Calibrating" instead of "No data" for new straps
Classic chart colours - a throwback toggle
Cleaner Live status + better sync diagnostics
Cleaner score rings + a few fixes
Clearer answers when your strap isn\'t banking history
Clearer labels, a journal fix, and steadier sleeping heart rate
Clearer sync status + a responsive Compare screen
Connection & sleep fixes - a focused tune-up
Continuous HRV capture - sharper overnight HRV, recovery and sleep
Continuous workouts no longer split, plus delete a sleep session
Coupled view, workouts rebuilt, journal numbers
Cross-platform parity - Android now scores identically to macOS & iOS
Date fixes, UI polish & clearer diagnostics
Date-hygiene fix for straps with a bad clock
Deep history backlog drains without manual taps
Deep sleep no longer reads 0 minutes, and a smarter AI Coach
Deep sleep that happens later in the night no longer reads 0 minutes
Deep Timeline you can scroll through days, faster manual workouts, and a storage clean-up
Delete a sleep, swipe to mark read
Dynamic Island toggle now actually turns it off
Editable naps, a richer Trends report, and better debug export
Effort explains a calm-day zero - and scores on the 5.0/MG
Effort scale fix for imported data
Everything: a whole new look, hydration, automatic workout detection, and smarter sleep
Faster, smoother, more languages, and a big pile of fixes
Fewer false daytime sleeps + an Android sync button
Find your strap log in Settings (macOS)
First-run terms acknowledgment + an Explore chart fix
Fix: a night with a brief wake-up showed as separate naps
Fix: app crashing / won\'t open when Bluetooth is on
Fix: bonded but no live data (Android)
Fix: connecting a Polar H10 or other heart-rate strap
Fix: imported phone steps were being double-counted
Fix the Android freeze after a few nights of data
Fix: your day now follows your timezone, not UTC
Fixed: imported data and strap sync getting stuck on iOS
Fixed: iPhone import, and a stuck store now self-heals
Fixes: the experimental sleep toggle now works, steps calibration, manual workouts on WHOOP 5/MG, and a sane HRV reading
Fixes the Insights-tab crash, plus more accurate HRV
Hand-correct your sleep times + smaller backups
Health Connect: correct labels + workout types (Android)
Health Connect shows as Health Connect
History dates fixed for revived straps, gestures during sync, clearer pairing
Honest labelling for WHOOP 5/MG deep-data diagnostics
Hotfix - making a heart-rate strap active no longer crashes
HR from the optical waveform, an early-morning day rollover, and clearer terms
HRV that reads true, and a tidier workout list
Import fixes - both sources, all data types
iOS is now a direct download - no Mac or Xcode needed
iPhone import: handle iCloud and large export files
iPhone polish: What\'s New fits, Today cards align
iPhone Workouts table fits the screen
Journal logging + an Imperial/Metric units toggle
Large Apple Health imports no longer crash (iPhone/Mac)
Light theme - NOOP in warm paper & gold
Live heart rate lands on today\'s chart even when the strap\'s clock is off (Android)
Live heart rate that doesn\'t freeze
Local Oura ring support: use your Oura ring with no Oura app (beta)
Mac: recovery builds from your strap alone
Mac: strap-computed nights show in Sleep

Mi Band import + a big WHOOP 4.0 sleep fix
Mind - a daily mood check-in - and nutrition import
More realistic calories + import & chart fixes
More reliable Bluetooth on newer Android phones
New: a guide to how your Charge, Effort and Rest scores work
New: use an Apple Watch with NOOP
New: Week in review, a live body console, fresher charts and more
No more HR spike when you reopen the app
NOOP grows up: it\'s not just for WHOOP anymore
NOOP now tells you when your strap isn\'t saving history - and how to fix it
On-demand HRV, a haptic clock, sleep marks & more
Open a workout, see what it costs you, and share your trends
Personal vital baselines + Mac analytics parity
Polish - matched to the iPhone & Mac design
German, French & Spanish, pull-to-sync on Today, and a wave of polish
Optimal-strain alerts, faster history sync, and a wave of accuracy fixes
Power saving that protects your strap, a Gemini-powered coach on Android, and richer metric detail

Readiness, and the start of WHOOP 5/MG
Readiness shows its evidence, and a Health Connect distance fix

Respiratory rate & skin temp in the Trends report
Restart your strap, lighter on battery, and Health Connect on Android 13
Robust Apple Health import, marginal-radio HR mode, live HR graph
Run the AI Coach on your own model - including fully local
See Everything: the Deep Timeline, a sleep movement graph, and a big board-clear
Share strap logs, and a worn-status fix
Shortcuts-export duplicates fixed; nutrition & mood reach Android charts
Shortcuts on Mac, recovery in the Android notification
Skin temperature unblocked on Mac/iOS, plus export fixes
Sleep and recovery for WHOOP 4.0 straps on the firmware we couldn\'t read
Sleep, Charge and workouts, cleaned up
Sleep-debt, daytime stress, a recovery forecast, and day-by-day navigation
Sleep figures, HR zones, charging & calibration - a big community-driven update

Sleep: keep real nights when the strap comes off
Sleep, properly sorted, and an app that explains itself
Sleep stages heal themselves after a sync
Sleep that was stuck in the archive comes back
Smart alarm actually works on Android
Smart alarm: the time you set is the time that fires
Smoother, an Oura live-HR fix, and a big pile of improvements

Split sleep: every block counted, one night per day

Steadier connections, fixes, and a nicer sleep view

Switch between your WHOOP 4 and 5.0 (Mac + Android)
Switching between WHOOP straps now actually switches
Sync visibility + a sharper Stress timeline
The archived-sleep recovery now reaches Android too
The new look everywhere - plus sleep, Effort & Bluetooth fixes
The new Today screen comes to Android
The smoothness release: faster everywhere, plus a sleep memory fix
The Test Centre: help us fix YOUR specific problem
Tidier Today hero, strap renaming, smarter journal
Tidier workout names, correct Rest duration
Tidy your journal - remove and hide questions
Today\'s Effort goes live - plus sleep & alarm honesty
Today\'s Effort no longer drops to zero
Today tiles no longer cut their value to "10…"
Today tiles no longer truncate their value (Android)
Today trends stay within their window (Mac)
True battery %, a sync indicator, and HR on imported workouts
Two quick fixes: the Blue Titanium icon, and Mac "Your Cards"
Universal Mac build + iPhone import fix
v5 - the raw-signal release: NOOP reads the signal, on your device, free
Week in Review is honest about a half-finished week
WHOOP 5.0 history decoding comes to Android
WHOOP 5.0/MG buzz - the real command (matched byte-for-byte)
WHOOP 5.0/MG buzz - trying the right command (experimental)
WHOOP 5/MG deep data: live confirmation it\'s working
WHOOP 5/MG deep-sync decode + sleep & workout fixes
WHOOP 5/MG heart rate on Mac + a Readiness fix
WHOOP-style HRV, warm-ups counted, and clearer cards
Workout calories - for manual sessions and Health Connect imports
Workouts: correct source pill for Health Connect (Android)
Your Fitness Age, Vitality & Body Age
Your imported steps now show on the Today screen (Android)
Your macOS data is back — and full French
Your scores build over a few nights
Your whole day\'s heart rate, on the dashboard
Your WHOOP journal in Insights, clearer metric taps
This section is on the way.
Charge it and put it on
Your strap is connected and we\'re trying an experimental handshake to bring up live
Delete all of this device\'s data?
It compares your resting heart rate and recent activity against people your age.
SpO₂, respiratory rate and skin temperature are sleep-window
Weight, height and waist add a VO₂max estimate. They don\'t change the Fitness Age itself.
A 60-second snapshot of your beat-to-beat (R-R) intervals from the strap, cleaned
Insights read your journal and outcomes
Pre-filled from last night. Tap to confirm or change.
Show a Today card reminding you to log your journal
Tap a day to catch up
Can\'t connect - your strap\'s pairing was reset
WHOOP 5.0/MG pairs with one app at a time. If a scan finds nothing, unpair it in
An experimental picture of your beat-to-beat timing. Please read these first.
I understand this is an experimental wellness feature, not a medical device or a diagnosis.
This is a wellness visualization, not a screening test. It does not tell you to see a clinician and it names no condition. This is not legal or medical advice.
A standalone companion for your WHOOP. Everything stays on this phone: your history, your live stream, your numbers. Nothing is uploaded.
A transparent cardiorespiratory recipe that recovers deep and REM better than the older
Buzz the time on your strap
Charge, Effort and Rest, and how they differ from WHOOP
Checks GitHub for the latest version when you tap. Nothing else is sent.

Counter ticks per step. Leave at 1.0 unless your steps run high. On a WHOOP 5/MG they can run very high (10× or more), so this goes up to 30. Walk a known 1,000 steps and divide NOOP\'s count by the real count to get your value.
Extends the sky behind the whole Today screen, so lowering Card transparency lets it show through every card. Needs the day-cycle background on.
Feel the current time as a sequence of buzzes (#460). Does nothing unless your strap is connected.
Flags accepted, but the enable sequence doesn\'t start a separate live stream. The deep records arrive as part of the normal history sync (#494).
For a WHOOP 4.0, which sends no step count: NOOP estimates steps from motion, calibrated to your phone. Tap to see how close it is and adjust it.
GitHub: code, releases, issues and the wiki.
How see-through the cards (Heart Rate, Key Metrics, Recovery Vitals, …) are. Left = solid, right = clear.
Importing overwrites everything currently on this phone. Your old data is kept in a side file just in case. NOOP needs a relaunch for an import to take effect.
Keep screen on during a workout
Keeps streaming from your strap with an ongoing notification, even after you close NOOP. Turn off to disconnect when the app is closed.

Keeps the detailed beat-to-beat stream running all day and night, not just while a live screen is open, so NOOP captures much more for overnight HRV, recovery and sleep. Uses more battery (your strap streams heart rate continuously). Needs "Keep connected in the background" on.
%1$s type-0x2F historical-offload frame(s) seen outside our sync. These are history (e.g. another app pulling the strap\'s backlog), not a live R22 stream (#494).

Makes your WHOOP 5.0/MG advertise its heart rate as a standard Bluetooth HR sensor, so a Garmin (Edge/watch), Zwift or gym equipment can use it during a workout. Applied on the next connection (and immediately if connected); writes the strap\'s whoop_live_hr_in_adv_ind_pkt flag. Reversible. 5/MG only.
NOOP is not a medical device. It is for informational and personal-insight purposes only and is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure or prevent any condition. Talk to a clinician for medical advice.

On a 5/MG connection NOOP will send a puffin realtime-stream request after the handshake, and log what comes back. If you have a 5/MG strap, turning this on and sharing your strap log helps map the protocol. No effect on WHOOP 4.0.
Open-source BLE reverse-engineering work. Thank you.
Project home and source on GitHub
Recent changes and what to expect

Records the raw frames of each 5/MG history sync to a file on this phone, so you can share them and help NOOP learn to decode 5/MG sleep, recovery and strain. The file contains raw biometric frames (heart rate, R-R, skin temperature, motion) and the strap\'s own diagnostic text. Nothing leaves the phone unless you share it. Off by default.
Rename your strap\'s Bluetooth name, useful for a second-hand band. The strap
Restarts the roughly 4-night build-up for Charge and your HRV baseline from tonight. Use it if a bad first week set your baseline off. Your history stays.
Reviews each scored wake block for real evidence of getting up (walking cadence, a
Runs the continuous HRV stream only during your quiet hours window (22:00–07:00 by default), roughly halving the battery cost. Daytime Stress readings will be sparser.

Saves the last 24h of decoded sensor samples (heart rate, R-R, motion, steps and any 5/MG deep streams you\'ve unlocked) as one CSV you can share, for tinkering with your own data. Nothing leaves the phone unless you share it.
Share on-device signals with the Coach
Shows a soft sunrise, day, dusk and night scene behind the Today screen. Turn it off for a plain dark canvas. Your cards stay exactly as readable.
Sleep sorting, scores, recording, and where your numbers come from.
Some phones also need auto-start enabled — open that screen
Steps estimate calibration. %1$s. Opens the calibration screen.
This restarts the roughly 4-night build-up for Charge and your HRV baseline. Your history stays. Use it if a bad first week, like wearing it while sick, set your baseline off.
What each strap can read, and why some features differ.

Whole night is NOOP\'s default measure; Deep sleep pools HRV over slow-wave sleep only, reading lower and matching WHOOP. Switching re-scores your recent nights over the new window and takes effect right away once you have a few nights of data.
WHOOP 4.0 versus 5.0: what each can read and why

WHOOP 5/MG straps hand a fresh app only live heart rate. The official app switches on the deeper streams (high-rate HR + motion + history) by writing a set of feature flags, a sequence two independent projects have documented. With this on, the button below sends that exact sequence to your strap. Unlike everything else here it does write to the strap, but it\'s reversible (it only changes which data the strap emits) and is the same thing the official app does. Experimental: it may do nothing on your firmware.
A period is likely between %1$s and
Couldn\'t reopen this night. Try again.
If this sleep came only from an import and no raw samples are stored, it may not reappear.
No earlier night stored yet. Earlier nights sync in the morning.
No naps recorded for this day.
No stage breakdown for this night.
No stage data recorded for this night.
Recompute a night to clear its deletion marker and scan the available raw data again.
Sleep detection reran using the data available for this night.
This moves the night to a time with no recorded data. Stages can\'t be derived there, so it may show as empty until data covers it.
Your night data is in. Logging how you felt helps NOOP learn what drives your best recovery.
Not enough recent days to chart a trend yet. Keep wearing your strap to populate it.
The line traces your autonomic load across the waking day, scored
These are extra, on-demand HRV lenses computed from today\'s R-R intervals. They
We compare today\'s resting heart rate and HRV to your own 30-day
Baselines learned on-device over 14 days. Bars are an approximate read of each
Choose which tiles show on your Control Center and reorder them with the arrows.
%1$s, %2$s. Tap to pick a day, swipe to change day.
Each line is how many points that signal moved Charge versus sitting at your
Live now. Your scores are building.
No cardio load yet. Effort builds once your heart rate climbs into your effort
Squarer tiles with a trend graph under the bar.
A clean, shareable one-page PDF of your recovery, sleep, HRV, resting heart rate
The share sheet can save the PDF to Files, or send it on.
%1$s of 7 days had data this week
Sleep steadiness: Rest varied ±%1$s pts night to night.
How recovered you are, led by HRV versus your personal baseline.
Cardiovascular load for the day, on a 0-100 scale (was 0-21).
How restorative your sleep was: duration, efficiency, deep+REM, timing.
Heart rate recovery at %1$s, %2$s beats per minute
The change from your heart rate at the end of exercise. Positive values mean your heart rate fell; a dash means the strap did not record enough data around that minute.
Each line shows how many beats per minute your heart rate changed after exercise. Only high-intensity workouts with recorded post-workout heart rate are included.
Heart-rate recovery trend in beats per minute
This strap doesn\'t send SpO₂ over Bluetooth. Import a WHOOP export or Health Connect to see it.
This strap sends no raw respiration stream. Your estimated respiratory rate appears on the Health screen.
Sharing paused — Health Connect permission was revoked. Tap to re-grant.
Last share didn’t finish — NOOP will retry automatically.
